iteration_rule: Generate 2-3 variants, reject by silhouette and spatial logic first, then evaluate material quality. Never one-shot accept.
shared_negative_prompt: No text, no logo, no brand mark, no character, no mascot, no perfume bottle, no medicine vial, no lab bottle label, no cute terrarium, no soil clump mess, no oversized plant, no decorative flowers, no landscape background, no roots piercing glass, no random cables, no dashboard UI.

variants.label: Suspended Root Lens
variants.why: Least likely to become a bottle. A horizontal glass lens reads as a technical specimen object and gives roots more believable space.
variants.model_prompt: Single centered GLB-ready 3D asset, premium suspended root lens specimen, horizontal clear glass lens capsule with soft oval silhouette, compact dark mineral core floating in the center, delicate root network suspended between two transparent glass membranes, subtle cyan signal veins following the roots, three tiny warm amber signal buds attached to internal root endpoints, quiet scientific botanical archive object, luxury museum material, no background scene, no text, no logo, no characters, no mascot.

spatial_contract.glass_shell: horizontal oval glass lens, not a vertical bottle
spatial_contract.core: dark mineral core floats at center and anchors all roots
spatial_contract.roots: roots stay fully inside lens volume and never pierce glass
spatial_contract.signals: cyan veins follow root paths or membrane channels
spatial_contract.buds: three tiny amber buds attach to internal root endpoints

reject_if: looks like a contact lens case
core is missing or reads as dirt

variants.label: Oval Archive Vessel
variants.why: Most literal specimen vessel. Good if generated glass is clean, risky if it becomes a bottle.
variants.model_prompt: Single centered GLB-ready 3D asset, transparent oval archive vessel, one-piece clear glass shell with flattened base and rounded shoulders, compact dark graphite mineral garden core inside, delicate roots contained inside the vessel, cyan data veins running through roots and subtle glass channels, three tiny amber signal buds attached to the internal core, quiet museum specimen, premium material separation, no text, no label, no logo, no characters, no mascot.

spatial_contract.glass_shell: oval glass vessel with no neck, cap, label, or product-bottle cues
spatial_contract.core: compact mineral garden core sits inside lower middle
spatial_contract.roots: roots curve around core inside vessel walls
spatial_contract.signals: cyan routes are internal and sparse
spatial_contract.buds: amber buds remain tiny signal nodes, not flowers

reject_if: looks like perfume, medicine, candle, or bottle packaging

variants.label: Core Reliquary
variants.why: Most premium and sculptural, but it risks drifting into a decorative object rather than technical specimen.
variants.model_prompt: Single centered GLB-ready 3D asset, glass and graphite botanical reliquary, asymmetrical clear glass shell around a dark mineral core, root filaments suspended like preserved scientific material, sparse cyan signal veins, three tiny amber data buds locked into small sockets, refined museum object, technical archive specimen, clean silhouette, no religious symbol, no text, no logo, no characters, no mascot.

spatial_contract.glass_shell: asymmetrical protective glass shell with visible thickness
spatial_contract.core: dark mineral core is sculptural but still technical
spatial_contract.roots: root filaments are preserved inside shell, not decorative vines
spatial_contract.signals: cyan veins stay sparse and precise
spatial_contract.buds: amber buds appear as data sockets

reject_if: roots read as vines wrapped outside
